 *我们目前仅在湾区为房屋提供服务，并计划在今年夏天扩展到其他城市。
  代表杰出组织（包括公司，品牌，非营利组织和媒体组织）的杰出执行官，思想领袖，影响者，作家和新闻工作者的房主。是政府官员的房主。运动员或职业电子竞技联赛的房主，以及官方团队网站上列出的教练，或在向新闻媒体（如Kotaku，Polygon或IGN）提出申请之前的6个月内拥有3个或更多推荐人的房主。拥有娱乐公司（例如电影制片厂，电视网络和音乐实体）的房主，以及与作品相关的具有公共角色的房主。在IMDB个人资料中拥有至少5个制作功劳的演员的房主。申请人需要房屋所有权证明，并且将逐案评估其身份。
   蓝色检查房屋使用内部信号的混合来评估给定房主的真实性。我们还评估了与提交的第三方公共资源相关的一系列信号，以支持验证检测欺诈性提交的申请。如果您不再符合我们的标准，我们也可能会随时从您的家中取下蓝色徽章徽章。搬迁不会产生额外费用。
    对于经过验证的房主，BLUE CHECK HOMES安装团队将以2999.99美元的费用保护您自己的房屋自己的石膏男爵c。此项费用包括定制绘画波峰的背景以匹配您家的配色方案，以及免费咨询以讨论徽章的位置。 （我们不会更改验证符号上的蓝色阴影。）我们已投保。如果您购买第二套房产或搬家，则必须重新申请波峰。如果您将房屋出售给新房主，则无需拆除顶饰，因为这是您的历史遗产和荣誉的一部分。
 就上下文而言，我是一位艺术家，他在互联网上开玩笑，有时会开玩笑，让我们把它变成一种服务。文化，互联网虚荣文化和可怕的资本主义观念。从历史上看，在维多利亚时代的房屋上发现的装饰性徽章是标志着财富和重要性的标志，我认为如果今天也存在这种观念，那将是愚蠢的。但是，如果它存在于今天，您是否需要由第三方验证以表明状态？这是玩笑的由来。是的，我也认为经过验证的蓝房子徽标的想法很愚蠢。我也喜欢在星期五晚上制作有趣的便条纸，但我的媒介是为目标网页撰写讽刺性文章并创建产品发布会。我实际上也是一位雕塑家，会完全为某人造一个（关于定价的IDK）。如果我确实制造了一个，那么在外面走来走去的人不太可能会对某人房屋上的蓝色复选标记做出反应。他们可能永远找不到。这是真的吗？它介于真假之间。任何人都可以用石膏制作东西。任何人都可以申请验证。任何人都可以登录页面。是否有实体参与其中，并且该实体正在海湾地区附近推广吗？否。如果您认为这是一项全面的服务，请调查您在互联网上阅读的内容！而且，如果您是在互联网上开玩笑的艺术家，我们应该考虑添加这样的免责声明，因为并非每个人都能理解您的评论，并会分享您的笑话。 😢

note_en:
The blue verified badge on your house lets people outside know that you're an authentic public figure. To receive the blue check crest, there must be someone authentic and notable actively living in the house.
 The blue verified badge on your house lets people outside know that you're an authentic public figure. To receive the blue check crest, there must be someone authentic and notable actively living in the house.
 *We are currently only servicing homes in the Bay Area, with plans to expand to other cities this summer.
  Homeowners who are prominent executives, thought leaders, influencers, authors, and journalists who represent prominent organizations, including companies, brands, non-profit organizations, and media organizations.     Homeowners who are government officials.     Homeowners who are athletes or on professional esports leagues, and coaches listed on the official team website or who have 3 or more featured references within the 6 months prior to applying in news outlets such as Kotaku, Polygon, or IGN.     Homeowners who own entertainment companies, such as film studios, TV networks, and music entities as well as homeowners with public facing roles associated with their productions.     Homeowners who are actors with at least 5 production credits on their IMDB profile.     Applicants need proof of home ownership and their status will be assessed on a case by case basis.
   BLUE CHECK HOMES uses a mix of internal signals to evaluate the authenticity of a given homeowner. We also assess a range of signals associated with third-party public resources submitted in support of applications for verification to detect fraudulent submissions.     We may also remove the blue badge crest from your home at any point if you no longer meet our criteria. Removal will not incur additional charges.
    For verified homeowners, the BLUE CHECK HOMES installation team will secure your home's very own plaster baronial crest for a fee of $2999.99. This fee includes custom painting the background of the crest to match the color scheme of your home and a free consultation to discuss the placement of the badge. (We will not change the shade of blue on the verification symbol.) We are insured.     If you purchase a second property or move homes you will have to reapply for the crest. You do not need to remove the crest if you sell your home to new homeowners, as it's part of your historical legacy and honor.
 For context, I'm an artist who makes  random internet jokes that sometimes pokes fun of "let's turn this into a service" culture, internet vanity culture, and terrible capitalist ideas. Historically, decorative crests found on Victorian homes were a mark to signal wealth and importance and I thought it would be dumb if that concept also existed today. But if it existed today, would you need to be verified by a third party to signal status? Here's the  origin of the joke.     Yes, I ALSO THINK THE IDEA OF VERIFIED BLUE HOUSE BADGES IS DUMB. I also love to fabricate funny shitposts on a Friday night, but my medium is writing satirical copy for landing pages and creating product launches. I'm also actually a sculptor and would totally make one for someone (IDK about pricing).     If I do make one, it's unlikely anyone walking around outside would react to a blue check mark on someone's house. They will probably never find it.     So is this real? It's somewhere in between real and fake. Anyone can craft something out of plaster. Anyone can apply to be verified. Anyone can make a landing page. Is there an entity involved and is this being rolled out across Bay Area neighborhoods? No.     If you thought this was a full-fledged service, please investigate the things you read on the internet! And if you're an artist making jokes on the internet, we should consider adding disclaimers like this, because not everyone understands your commentary and will share your jokes as fact. 😢
  BLUE CHECK HOMES is not affiliated with Twitter and is run by a local sculptor in San Francisco.

 莫洛奇！莫洛奇！机器人公寓！看不见的郊区！骷髅国库！盲目的首都！恶魔产业！频谱国家！无敌的疯人院！花岗岩公鸡！巨大的炸弹！
 他们摔断了背把Moloch带到天堂！路面，树木，收音机，吨！将城市带到存在的无处不在的天堂！
   突破！河流之上！翻转和钉十字架！洪水淹没了！高潮！主显节！绝望！十年来的动物尖叫和自杀！注意！新爱！疯狂的一代！落在时间的岩石上！
 河中真正的圣笑！他们看到了一切！野性的眼睛！圣吼！他们告别！他们跳下屋顶！孤独！挥手！带花！下河了！上街！
 这首诗给我留下深刻的印象的是它把文明作为一个个体的概念。您几乎可以看到他，用他的军队的手指和他摩天大楼的眼睛。 
许多评论员说，莫洛奇代表资本主义。这绝对是其中的一部分，甚至很大。但这不太适合。资本主义的命运是无性别的氢云？资本主义是我没有身体的意识吗？资本主义，所以花岗石公鸡？
 莫洛奇（Moloch）是作为一个问题的答案而提出的-C. S. Lewis在《哲学家的层次》中的问题–是什么？地球可能是公平的，所有人都感到高兴和明智。相反，我们有监狱，烟囱和庇护所。水泥和铝的狮身人面像是什么打开了头骨并吞噬了他们的想象力？
  原则上有一段段落，其中马拉科利瑟向女神抱怨人类社会的弊端。 “每个人都在互相伤害，地球上充满了不公正现象，整个社会掠夺了自己的人民，母亲们监禁儿子，孩子们在兄弟之战中丧生。”
 女神回答：“这是怎么回事，如果这是你想做的？”
   隐含的问题是–如果每个人都讨厌当前的系统，谁会永久保留它？金斯伯格回答：“莫洛克”。它之所以强大，不是因为它是正确的-没有人从字面上认为古老的迦太基恶魔会导致一切-而是因为将系统视为代理人，使该系统不是代理人的程度大为减轻。
 博斯特罗姆无意中提到了无独裁者的反乌托邦的可能性，包括领导层在内的每个公民都讨厌这种反乌托邦，但这种耐心却永无止境。想象这样的状态很容易。想象一个有两个规则的国家：首先，每个人每天必须花费八小时给自己强烈的电击。第二，如果任何人不遵守某项规则（包括该规则），或大声疾呼，或不执行该规则，则所有公民必须团结起来杀死该人。假设这些规则已经由每个人都希望它们得到执行的传统所确立。
 因此，您一天要震惊八小时，因为您知道如果不这样做，其他所有人都会杀死您，因为如果不这样做，其他所有人都会杀死他们，依此类推。每个公民都讨厌这个系统，但是由于缺乏良好的协调机制，它可以忍受。从上帝的角度来看，我们可以将系统优化为“每个人都同意立即停止执行此操作”，但是系统中没有人能够在没有很大风险的情况下实现过渡。 
好的，这个例子是人为的。因此，让我们看一看-假设有十个-真实世界中类似多极阱的例子，以真正说明这是多么重要。
 1.囚徒困境，由两个非常愚蠢的自由主义者扮演，他们最终以缺陷缺陷告终。如果他们能弄清楚协调的话，会有更好的结果，但是协调很难。从上帝的角度来看，我们可以同意合作-合作比缺陷缺陷更好的结果，但是系统中的任何囚犯都无法实现。
 2.美元拍卖。我在《游戏理论：一门黑暗的艺术》中写了这个原理，甚至更复杂的版本。使用一些奇怪的拍卖规则，您可以利用协调不力的优势，使某人为一张一美元的钞票支付10美元。从上帝的角度来看，很明显，人们不应该为车载电视支付10美元。从系统内部看，每个单独的步骤可能都是合理的。
   作为一项思想实验，让我们考虑一下湖中的水产养殖（养鱼）。想象一下一个湖泊，其中有一千个相同的养鱼场，由一千个竞争公司拥有。每个养鱼场每月可获利$ 1000。一会儿，一切都很好。
 但是每个养鱼场都会产生废物，这会污染湖中的水。假设每个养鱼场产生的污染足以使湖中的生产力降低1美元/月。
 一千个养鱼场产生的废物足以使生产力降低1000美元/月，这意味着没有一个养鱼场能赚钱。资本主义救助：有人发明了一种复杂的过滤系统，可以去除废物。每月费用为$ 300。所有的养鱼场都自愿安装它，污染结束了，养鱼场现在每月可获利700美元，这仍然是一笔可观的数目。
 但是，一个农夫（叫他史蒂夫（Steve））厌倦了花钱来操作他的过滤器。现在，一个值得浪费的养鱼场正在污染湖泊，使生产力降低了1美元。史蒂夫（Steve）可获得999美元的利润，其他人则可获得699美元的利润。 
每个人都看到史蒂夫的利润比他们要多得多，因为他没有在过滤器上花费维护成本。他们也断开了过滤器的连接。
 一旦有四百人断开过滤器，史蒂夫每月的收入为600美元–比他和其他所有人都打开过滤器的收入要低！贫穷的良性过滤器用户只赚300美元。史蒂夫向所有人走来，说：“等等！我们都需要自愿签署协议才能使用过滤器！否则，每个人的生产力都会下降。”
 每个人都同意他的观点，他们都签署了《过滤器契约》，除了一个有点混蛋的人。我们叫他迈克。现在，除了迈克，每个人都再次使用过滤器。 Mike的月薪为999美元，其他所有人的月薪为699美元。慢慢地，人们开始认为他们也应该像迈克一样赚大钱，并断开过滤器以获得300美元的额外利润……
 自私的人永远不会有使用过滤器的动机。一个自私的人有一定的动机去签署一项协议，以使每个人都使用过滤器，但是在许多情况下，动机更强的是等待其他人签署这样的协议而退出自己。这可能导致不希望的平衡，在这种平衡中没有人会签署这样的协定。
 我想得越多，我就越觉得这是我反对自由主义者的核心，而非自由主义者的FAQ 3.0就是这个例子，被复制粘贴了200次。从上帝的角度来看，我们可以说污染湖泊会导致严重的后果。在系统内部，没有人可以防止湖泊受到污染，因此购买过滤器可能不是一个好主意。
 4.马尔萨斯陷阱，至少在其极其纯粹的理论极限上。假设您是最早被引入原始岛屿的老鼠之一。那里到处都是美味的植物，您过着田园诗般的生活，四处闲逛，吃东西并创作精美的艺术品（您是NIMH的那只老鼠之一）。
 您的伴侣寿命很长，并且有十几个孩子。他们所有人都有十几个孩子，依此类推。几代人以来，该岛拥有一万只老鼠，并已达到其承载能力。现在没有足够的食物和空间，为了维持一万人口的稳定，每一代人都会死掉一定比例的人口。 
某些大鼠宗派放弃了艺术，以将更多的时间投入到生存中。这一世代的死亡比主流世代少得多，直到一段时间后，再也没有老鼠能构成任何艺术品，任何试图将其带回的老鼠都将在几代内灭绝。
 实际上，这不只是艺术。任何比主流更精简，卑鄙和更加生存的派别最终都会接管。如果一只老鼠无私地决定将其后代限制为每对夫妇两只，以减少种群过多，那该家伙将死掉，并因其更多的敌人而蜂拥而至。如果老鼠的一个教派开始实行自相残杀，并发现它比同伴有优势，它最终将接管并达到固视。
 如果一些老鼠科学家预测该岛坚果库的枯竭速度正在以危险的速度加剧，并且很快将其全部消耗exhaust尽，那么一些派别的老鼠可能会试图将其坚果消耗量限制在可持续的水平。那些老鼠会被他们更自私的表亲击败。最终，坚果将耗尽，大部分老鼠会死掉，并且循环将再次开始。任何鼓吹某种动作来停止周期的老鼠都会被他们的表兄弟所竞争，他们的表兄弟们鼓吹任何东西都是在浪费时间，这些时间可以用来竞争和消耗。
 出于多种原因，进化并不是理想情况下的马尔萨斯式，但它提供了原型示例，我们可以将其应用于其他事物以查看其潜在机制。从神的角度来看，很容易说出老鼠应该保持舒适的低种群。从系统内部，每只老鼠都将遵循其遗传要求，该岛将陷入无尽的繁荣与萧条循环。
 5.资本主义。想象一下一个残酷行业的资本家。他雇用一家血汗工厂的工人缝制服装，而这些服装使他的利润微乎其微。也许他想付给他的工人更多的薪水，或者给他们更好的工作条件。但是他不能，因为那会提高他的产品价格，而且他会被更便宜的竞争对手打败，然后破产。也许他的许多竞争对手都是很乐意的人，他们愿意给他们的工人更多的报酬，但是除非他们有某种坚定的保证，否则他们中的任何一个都不会因降低价格而背叛他们，所以他们做不到。
 像老鼠一样，除了纯粹的竞争之外，它们逐渐失去所有价值，因此处于竞争激烈的经济环境中的公司被迫放弃所有价值（除了以盈利为目的的优化之外），否则将被为利润更好而进行优化的公司竞争，因此可以出售同样的服务，价格更低。
 （我不太确定人们能欣赏到将资本主义模拟为进化的价值的广泛程度。合适的公司（定义为使客户希望从他们那里购买产品的公司）生存，扩展并激发未来的努力，而不合适的公司（定义为那些没人要买的人–破产，连同他们的公司DNA一起灭亡。红色的原因，牙齿和爪子的原因与市场残酷和剥削的原因相同） 
从上帝的角度来看，我们可以构建一个友好的行业，每个公司都以其工资来支付工人的生活费。从系统内部无法执行它。
  6.最近在此博客上讨论的两次收入陷阱。从理论上讲，对好学区郊区房屋的激烈竞争意味着人们不得不放弃许多其他价值观，例如与子女在一起的时间，财务保障，以优化购房能力，否则将其委托给贫民窟。
 从所有人的角度来看，如果每个人都同意不承担第二份工作来帮助他们赢得漂亮房子的竞争，那么每个人都会得到和以前一样好的房子，而只需要工作一份即可。在系统内部，没有一个真正愿意禁止第二份工作的政府，所有找不到工作的人都会被抛在后面。
  7.农业。贾里德·戴蒙德（Jared Diamond）称其为人类历史上最严重的错误。不管这是不是一个错误，这都不是偶然的事-农业文明只是在不可避免和不可抗拒的情况下胜过了游牧民族。经典的马尔萨斯陷阱。也许狩猎聚会更令人愉快，寿命更长，更有利于人类的繁荣发展，但在人民之间的竞争十分激烈的情况下，农业以其所有疾病，压迫和瘟疫是更具竞争力的选择，每个人都将结束农民或走科曼奇印第安人的道路。
 从上帝的眼光看，很容易看到每个人都应该保留更愉快的选择并留在狩猎者那里。从系统内部来看，每个部落只面临着农业化或不可避免地死亡的选择。
 8.军备竞赛。大国可以将其预算的5％到30％用于国防。在没有战争的情况下（过去50年来一直保持这种状态），这一切只会使基础建设，医疗，教育或经济增长损失大量资金。但是，任何未能在国防上投入足够资金的国家都可能遭到这样做的邻国的入侵。因此，几乎所有国家都试图在国防上花费一些钱。
 从上帝的角度来看，最好的解决方案是世界和平，没有哪个国家拥有军队。在系统内部，任何国家都不能单方面执行该命令，因此，他们的最佳选择是继续将其资金投入到没有使用的筒仓中的导弹上。 
9.癌症。人体应该由和谐生活的细胞组成，并为生物的更大利益集中其资源。如果一个细胞通过投入其自身的资源来复制自身而偏离了这种平衡，那么它及其后代就会蓬勃发展，最终胜过所有其他细胞，并接管整个身体，从而死亡。否则情况可能会重复，某些癌细胞会逆转其余肿瘤，从而减慢其生长并导致肿瘤停滞。
 从上帝的角度来看，最好的解决方案是所有细胞相互协作，以免死亡。从系统内部，癌细胞会扩散并胜过另一个细胞-因此，只有免疫系统的存在才能阻止癌症的自然诱因。
 10.“自下而上”描述了一种政治局势，其中一些司法管辖区承诺降低税率和减少法规以引诱企业。最终结果是，每个人要么通过最低的税率和法规来优化竞争力，要么将所有业务，收入和工作流失给做到这一点的人（此时，他们将被淘汰，并由一个将更加合规）。
 但是，即使最后一个窃取了名称，但实际上所有这些情况都是底线竞赛。一旦一个代理商学会了如何通过牺牲共同的价值来提高竞争力，其所有竞争者也必须牺牲这一价值，或者被竞争者所取代，并被不那么认真的人所取代。因此，该系统很可能最终使每个人再次具有同等的竞争力，但是牺牲的价值将永远消失。从上帝的角度来看，竞争者知道如果他们叛逃，他们都会变得更糟，但是从系统内部来看，由于协调不足，无法避免。
 在我们继续之前，有一种略有不同的多代理陷阱形式值得研究。在这个竞赛中，竞争是通过某种外部力量（通常是社会耻辱）来阻止的。结果，实际上并没有达到底线的竞争-系统可以在相对较高的水平上继续运行-但无法进行优化，并且资源无缘无故地被丢弃。以免您在我们开始之前就筋疲力尽，在这里我仅举四个例子。
 11.教育。在关于反动哲学的文章中，我谈到了我对教育改革的沮丧：
 人们问为什么我们不能改革教育制度。但是现在，学生的动力是去他们能进入的最负盛名的大学，以便雇主雇用他们-无论他们是否学到任何东西。雇主的动机是让学生从最负盛名的大学毕业，以便他们在错误的情况下可以向老板捍卫自己的决定-无论大学是否提供增值服务。而大学的动力在于尽一切可能获得更多声望（以《美国新闻与世界报道》的排名衡量），不管它是否对学生有帮助。这会导致巨大的浪费和不良的教育吗？是。教育神能注意到这一点，并制定一些教育法令来导致效率大大提高吗？容易！但是，由于没有教育神，每个人都只会遵循自己的动机，而动机与教育或效率只是部分相关。 
从上帝的角度来看，很容易说出这样的话：“学生只有在认为自己可以从中学到东西时才应该上大学，而雇主应该根据他们的能力而不是根据他们上的大学来雇用申请人”。在系统内部，每个人都已经正确地遵循了自己的激励措施，因此，除非激励措施改变了系统，否则也不会。
  现代研究界知道，他们没有创造出最好的科学。出版方面存在很多偏见，出于纯粹的惯性，统计数据以一种令人困惑和误导的方式进行，而且复制往往发生得很晚或根本没有发生。有时有人会说类似“我不敢相信人们愚蠢到无法修复科学。我们要做的就是要求及早注册研究，以避免发表偏见，将这种强大的新统计技术转变为新标准，并为从事复制实验的科学家赋予更高的地位。这将非常简单，并且将大大促进科学进步。我必须比所有现有的科学家都聪明，因为我能够想到这一点，但事实并非如此。”
 是的这将对科学之神起作用。他可以制定一项所有人都必须使用正确的统计数据的科学法令，并制定另一个所有人都必须赋予复制品更高地位的科学法令。
 但是，从上帝的角度来看起作用的事情在系统内部是行不通的。没有任何一个科学家有动机单方面转向自己的研究使用新的统计技术，因为这会使她的研究不太可能产生令人震惊的结果，并且只会使所有其他科学家感到困惑。他们只是有动力要其他人去做，这时他们就会跟着做。而且没有个人日记

note_en:
What sphinx of cement and aluminum bashed open their skulls and ate up their brains and imagination?
 Moloch! Solitude! Filth! Ugliness! Ashcans and unobtainable dollars! Children screaming under the stairways! Boys sobbing in armies! Old men weeping in the parks!
 Moloch! Moloch! Nightmare of Moloch! Moloch the loveless! Mental Moloch! Moloch the heavy judger of men!
 Moloch the incomprehensible prison! Moloch the crossbone soulless jailhouse and Congress of sorrows! Moloch whose buildings are judgment! Moloch the vast stone of war! Moloch the stunned governments!
 Moloch whose mind is pure machinery! Moloch whose blood is running money! Moloch whose fingers are ten armies! Moloch whose breast is a cannibal dynamo! Moloch whose ear is a smoking tomb!
 Moloch whose eyes are a thousand blind windows! Moloch whose skyscrapers stand in the long streets like endless Jehovahs! Moloch whose factories dream and croak in the fog! Moloch whose smoke-stacks and antennae crown the cities!
 Moloch whose love is endless oil and stone! Moloch whose soul is electricity and banks! Moloch whose poverty is the specter of genius! Moloch whose fate is a cloud of sexless hydrogen! Moloch whose name is the Mind!
 Moloch in whom I sit lonely! Moloch in whom I dream Angels! Crazy in Moloch! Cocksucker in Moloch! Lacklove and manless in Moloch!
 Moloch who entered my soul early! Moloch in whom I am a consciousness without a body! Moloch who frightened me out of my natural ecstasy! Moloch whom I abandon! Wake up in Moloch! Light streaming out of the sky!
 Moloch! Moloch! Robot apartments! invisible suburbs! skeleton treasuries! blind capitals! demonic industries! spectral nations! invincible madhouses! granite cocks! monstrous bombs!
 They broke their backs lifting Moloch to Heaven! Pavements, trees, radios, tons! lifting the city to Heaven which exists and is everywhere about us!
   Breakthroughs! over the river! flips and crucifixions! gone down the flood! Highs! Epiphanies! Despairs! Ten years’ animal screams and suicides! Minds! New loves! Mad generation! down on the rocks of Time!
 Real holy laughter in the river! They saw it all! the wild eyes! the holy yells! They bade farewell! They jumped off the roof! to solitude! waving! carrying flowers! Down to the river! into the street!
 What’s always impressed me about this poem is its conception of civilization as an individual entity. You can almost see him, with his fingers of armies and his skyscraper-window eyes.
 A lot of the commentators say Moloch represents capitalism. This is definitely a piece of it, even a big piece. But it doesn’t quite fit. Capitalism, whose fate is a cloud of sexless hydrogen? Capitalism in whom I am a consciousness without a body? Capitalism, therefore granite cocks?
 Moloch is introduced as the answer to a question – C. S. Lewis’ question in  Hierarchy Of Philosophers –  what does it? Earth could be fair, and all men glad and wise. Instead we have prisons, smokestacks, asylums. What sphinx of cement and aluminum breaks open their skulls and eats up their imagination?
  There’s  a passage in the  Principia Discordia where Malaclypse complains to the Goddess about the evils of human society. “Everyone is hurting each other, the planet is rampant with injustices, whole societies plunder groups of their own people, mothers imprison sons, children perish while brothers war.”
 The Goddess answers: “What is the matter with that, if it’s what you want to do?”
   The implicit question is – if everyone hates the current system, who perpetuates it? And Ginsberg answers: “Moloch”. It’s powerful not because it’s correct – nobody literally thinks an ancient Carthaginian demon causes everything – but because thinking of the system as an agent throws into relief the degree to which the system  isn’t an agent.
 Bostrom makes an offhanded reference of the possibility of a dictatorless dystopia, one that every single citizen including the leadership hates but which nevertheless endures unconquered. It’s easy enough to imagine such a state. Imagine a country with two rules: first, every person must spend eight hours a day giving themselves strong electric shocks. Second, if anyone fails to follow a rule (including this one), or speaks out against it, or fails to enforce it, all citizens must unite to kill that person. Suppose these rules were well-enough established by tradition that everyone expected them to be enforced.
 So you shock yourself for eight hours a day, because you know if you don’t everyone else will kill you, because if they don’t, everyone else will kill  them, and so on. Every single citizen hates the system, but for lack of a good coordination mechanism it endures. From a god’s-eye-view, we can optimize the system to “everyone agrees to stop doing this at once”, but no one within the system is able to effect the transition without great risk to themselves.
 And okay, this example is kind of contrived. So let’s run through – let’s say ten – real world examples of similar multipolar traps to really hammer in how important this is.
 1. The Prisoner’s Dilemma, as played by two very dumb libertarians who keep ending up on defect-defect. There’s a much better outcome available if they could figure out the coordination, but coordination is  hard. From a god’s-eye-view, we can agree that cooperate-cooperate is a better outcome than defect-defect, but neither prisoner within the system can make it happen.
 2. Dollar auctions. I wrote about this and even more convoluted versions of the same principle in  Game Theory As A Dark Art. Using some  weird auction rules, you can take advantage of poor coordination to make someone pay $10 for a one dollar bill. From a god’s-eye-view, clearly people should not pay $10 for a on-er. From within the system, each individual step taken might be rational.
   As a thought experiment, let’s consider aquaculture (fish farming) in a lake. Imagine a lake with a thousand identical fish farms owned by a thousand competing companies. Each fish farm earns a profit of $1000/month. For a while, all is well.
 But each fish farm produces waste, which fouls the water in the lake. Let’s say each fish farm produces enough pollution to lower productivity in the lake by $1/month.
 A thousand fish farms produce enough waste to lower productivity by $1000/month, meaning none of the fish farms are making any money. Capitalism to the rescue: someone invents a complex filtering system that removes waste products. It costs $300/month to operate. All fish farms voluntarily install it, the pollution ends, and the fish farms are now making a profit of $700/month – still a respectable sum.
 But one farmer (let’s call him Steve) gets tired of spending the money to operate his filter. Now one fish farm worth of waste is polluting the lake, lowering productivity by $1. Steve earns $999 profit, and everyone else earns $699 profit.
 Everyone else sees Steve is much more profitable than they are, because he’s not spending the maintenance costs on his filter. They disconnect their filters too.
 Once four hundred people disconnect their filters, Steve is earning $600/month – less than he would be if he and everyone else had kept their filters on! And the poor virtuous filter users are only making $300. Steve goes around to everyone, saying “Wait! We all need to make a voluntary pact to use filters! Otherwise, everyone’s productivity goes down.”
 Everyone agrees with him, and they all sign the Filter Pact, except one person who is sort of a jerk. Let’s call him Mike. Now everyone is back using filters again, except Mike. Mike earns $999/month, and everyone else earns $699/month. Slowly, people start thinking they too should be getting big bucks like Mike, and disconnect their filter for $300 extra profit…
 A self-interested person never has any incentive to use a filter. A self-interested person has some incentive to sign a pact to make everyone use a filter, but in many cases has a stronger incentive to wait for everyone else to sign such a pact but opt out himself. This can lead to an undesirable equilibrium in which no one will sign such a pact.
 The more I think about it, the more I feel like this is the core of my objection to libertarianism, and that Non-Libertarian FAQ 3.0 will just be this one example copy-pasted two hundred times. From a god’s-eye-view, we can say that polluting the lake leads to bad consequences. From within the system, no individual can prevent the lake from being polluted, and buying a filter might not be such a good idea.
 4. The Malthusian trap, at least at its extremely pure theoretical limits. Suppose you are one of the first rats introduced onto a pristine island. It is full of yummy plants and you live an idyllic life lounging about, eating, and composing great works of art (you’re one of those rats from   The Rats of NIMH ).
 You live a long life, mate, and have a dozen children. All of them have a dozen children, and so on. In a couple generations, the island has ten thousand rats and has reached its carrying capacity. Now there’s not enough food and space to go around, and a certain percent of each new generation dies in order to keep the population steady at ten thousand.
 A certain sect of rats abandons art in order to devote more of their time to scrounging for survival. Each generation, a bit less of this sect dies than members of the mainstream, until after a while, no rat composes any art at all, and any sect of rats who try to bring it back will go extinct within a few generations.
 In fact, it’s not just art. Any sect at all that is leaner, meaner, and more survivalist than the mainstream will eventually take over. If one sect of rats altruistically decides to limit its offspring to two per couple in order to decrease overpopulation, that sect will die out, swarmed out of existence by its more numerous enemies. If one sect of rats starts practicing cannibalism, and finds it gives them an advantage over their fellows, it will eventually take over and reach fixation.
 If some rat scientists predict that depletion of the island’s nut stores is accelerating at a dangerous rate and they will soon be exhausted completely, a few sects of rats might try to limit their nut consumption to a sustainable level. Those rats will be outcompeted by their more selfish cousins. Eventually the nuts will be exhausted, most of the rats will die off, and the cycle will begin again. Any sect of rats advocating some action to stop  the cycle will be outcompeted by their cousins for whom advocating  anything is a waste of time that could be used to compete and consume.
 For a bunch of reasons evolution is not quite as Malthusian as the ideal case, but it provides the prototype example we can apply to other things to see the underlying mechanism. From a god’s-eye-view, it’s easy to say the rats should maintain a comfortably low population. From within the system, each individual rat will follow its genetic imperative and the island will end up in an endless boom-bust cycle.
 5. Capitalism. Imagine a capitalist in a cutthroat industry. He employs workers in a sweatshop to sew garments, which he sells at minimal profit. Maybe he would like to pay his workers more, or give them nicer working conditions. But he can’t, because that would raise the price of his products and he would be outcompeted by his cheaper rivals and go bankrupt. Maybe many of his rivals are nice people who would like to pay their workers more, but unless they have some kind of ironclad guarantee that none of them are going to defect by undercutting their prices they can’t do it.
 Like the rats, who gradually lose all values except sheer competition, so companies in an economic environment of  sufficiently intense competition are forced to abandon all values except optimizing-for-profit or else be outcompeted by companies that optimized for profit better and so can sell the same service at a lower price.
 (I’m not really sure how widely people appreciate the value of analogizing capitalism to evolution. Fit companies – defined as those that make the customer want to buy from them – survive, expand, and inspire future efforts, and unfit companies – defined as those no one wants to buy from – go bankrupt and die out along with their  company DNA. The reasons Nature is red and tooth and claw are the same reasons the market is ruthless and exploitative)
 From a god’s-eye-view, we can contrive a friendly industry where every company pays its workers a living wage. From within the system, there’s no way to enact it.
  6.  The Two-Income Trap, as recently discussed on this blog. It theorized that sufficiently intense competition for suburban houses in good school districts meant that people had to throw away lots of other values – time at home with their children, financial security – to optimize for house-buying-ability or else be consigned to the ghetto.
 From a god’s-eye-view, if everyone agrees not to take on a second job to help win their competition for nice houses, then everyone will get exactly as nice a house as they did before, but only have to work one job. From within the system, absent a government literally willing to ban second jobs, everyone who doesn’t get one will be left behind.
  7. Agriculture. Jared Diamond calls it  the worst mistake in human history. Whether or not it was a mistake, it wasn’t an  accident – agricultural civilizations simply outcompeted nomadic ones, inevitable and irresistably. Classic Malthusian trap. Maybe hunting-gathering was more enjoyable, higher life expectancy, and more conducive to human flourishing – but in a state of  sufficiently intense competition between peoples, in which agriculture with all its disease and oppression and pestilence was the more competitive option, everyone will end up agriculturalists or  go the way of the Comanche Indians.
 From a god’s-eye-view, it’s easy to see everyone should keep the more enjoyable option and stay hunter-gatherers. From within the system, each individual tribe only faces the choice of going agricultural or inevitably dying.
 8. Arms races. Large countries can spend anywhere from 5% to 30% of their budget on defense. In the absence of war – a condition which has mostly held for the past fifty years – all this does is sap money away from infrastructure, health, education, or economic growth. But any country that fails to spend enough money on defense risks being invaded by a neighboring country that did. Therefore, almost all countries try to spend some money on defense.
 From a god’s-eye-view, the best solution is world peace and no country having an army at all. From within the system, no country can unilaterally enforce that, so their best option is to keep on throwing their money into missiles that lie in silos unused.
  9. Cancer. The human body is supposed to be made up of cells living harmoniously and pooling their resources for the greater good of the organism. If a cell defects from this equilibrium by investing its resources into copying itself, it and its descendants will flourish, eventually outcompeting all the other cells and taking over the body – at which point it dies. Or the situation may repeat, with certain cancer cells defecting against the rest of the tumor, thus slowing down its growth and causing the tumor to stagnate.
 From a god’s-eye-view, the best solution is all cells cooperating so that they don’t all die. From within the system, cancerous cells will proliferate and outcompete the other – so that only the existence of the immune system keeps the natural incentive to turn cancerous in check.
 10. The “race to the bottom” describes  a political situation where some jurisdictions lure businesses by promising lower taxes and fewer regulations. The end result is that either everyone optimizes for competitiveness – by having minimal tax rates and regulations – or they lose all of their business, revenue, and jobs to people who did (at which point they are pushed out and replaced by a government who will be more compliant).
 But even though the last one has stolen the name, all these scenarios are in fact a race to the bottom. Once one agent learns how to become more competitive by sacrificing a common value, all its competitors must also sacrifice that value or be outcompeted and replaced by the less scrupulous. Therefore, the system is likely to end up with everyone once again equally competitive, but the sacrificed value is gone forever. From a god’s-eye-view, the competitors know they will all be worse off if they defect, but from within the system, given insufficient coordination it’s impossible to avoid.
 Before we go on, there’s a slightly different form of multi-agent trap worth investigating. In this one, the competition is kept at bay by some outside force – usually social stigma. As a result, there’s not actually a race to the bottom – the system can continue functioning at a relatively high level – but it’s impossible to optimize and resources are consistently thrown away for no reason. Lest you get exhausted before we even begin, I’ll limit myself to four examples here.
 11. Education. In my essay on reactionary philosophy, I talk about my frustration with education reform:
 People ask why we can’t reform the education system. But right now students’ incentive is to go to the most prestigious college they can get into so employers will hire them – whether or not they learn anything. Employers’ incentive is to get students from the most prestigious college they can so that they can defend their decision to their boss if it goes wrong – whether or not the college provides value added. And colleges’ incentive is to do whatever it takes to get more prestige, as measured in  US News and World Report rankings – whether or not it helps students. Does this lead to huge waste and poor education? Yes. Could the Education God notice this and make some Education Decrees that lead to a vastly more efficient system? Easily! But since there’s no Education God everybody is just going to follow their own incentives, which are only partly correlated with education or efficiency.
 From a god’s eye view, it’s easy to say things like “Students should only go to college if they think they will get something out of it, and employers should hire applicants based on their competence and not on what college they went to”. From within the system, everyone’s already following their own incentives correctly, so unless the incentives change the system won’t either.
  The modern research community  knows they aren’t producing the best science they could be. There’s lots of publication bias, statistics are done in a confusing and misleading way out of sheer inertia, and replications often happen very late or not at all. And sometimes someone will say something like “I can’t believe people are too dumb to fix Science. All we would have to do is require early registration of studies to avoid publication bias, turn this new and powerful statistical technique into the new standard, and accord higher status to scientists who do replication experiments. It would be really simple and it would vastly increase scientific progress. I must just be smarter than all existing scientists, since I’m able to think of this and they aren’t.”
 And yeah. That would work for the Science God. He could just make a Science Decree that everyone has to use the right statistics, and make another Science Decree that everyone must accord replications higher status.
 But things that work from a god’s-eye view don’t work from within the system. No individual scientist has an incentive to unilaterally switch to the new statistical technique for her own research, since it would make her research less likely to produce earth-shattering results and since it would just confuse all the other scientists. They just have an incentive to want everybody else to do it, at which point they would follow along. And no individual journal h

您可能不知道，但是您内部生活着一大群微生物，这些微生物对于抵御各种威胁（包括导致COVID-19的病毒）至关重要。
 在过去的二十年中，科学家们知道我们的身体比人类的细菌细胞更多。这个生活在我们体内和之上的细菌群落称为微生物组，类似于一家公司，每种微生物都从事专门的工作，但所有微生物都在保持我们的健康。在肠道中，细菌平衡了对病原体的免疫反应。这些细菌可确保免疫反应有效，但强度不高，不会对宿主造成附带损害。
 我们肠道中的细菌可以引发针对不仅感染肠道病毒（例如诺如病毒和轮状病毒）而且感染肺部的病毒（如流感病毒）的有效免疫反应。有益的肠道微生物通过命令专门的免疫细胞产生有效的抗病毒蛋白来最终消除病毒感染。缺乏这些有益的肠道细菌的人的身体对入侵病毒的免疫反应不会那么强。结果，感染可能无法得到控制，从而损害了健康。
 我是一位微生物学家，着迷于细菌塑造人类健康的方式。我研究的一个重要重点是弄清楚填充我们肠道的有益细菌如何抵抗疾病和感染。我最近的工作集中于患者特定微生物与COVID-19严重程度之间的联系。我的最终目标是弄清楚如何通过饮食来增强肠道微生物组，以引起强烈的免疫反应-不仅针对SARS-CoV-2，还包括所有病原体。
   我们的免疫防御是针对有害病原体（例如病毒或细菌）的复杂生物反应的一部分。但是，由于我们的身体居住着数以万亿计的有益细菌，病毒和真菌，因此我们对免疫反应的激活进行了严格的调节，以区分有害微生物和有益微生物。
 我们的细菌是与众不同的同伴，他们勤奋地帮助我们增强免疫系统的防御能力以抵抗感染。一项开创性研究发现，用抗生素处理过的可消除肠道细菌的小鼠表现出受损的免疫反应。这些动物的抗病毒白细胞数量低，抗体反应弱，蛋白质产生能力差，这对抵抗病毒感染和调节免疫反应至关重要。
 在另一项研究中，给小鼠喂食了乳酸菌细菌，这种细菌通常在发酵食品中用作益生菌。这些微生物降低了流感感染的严重性。与未经治疗的小鼠相比，经乳杆菌治疗的小鼠没有体重减轻，并且仅有轻度的肺损伤。同样，其他人发现用乳杆菌治疗小鼠可预防流感病毒和人类呼吸道合胞病毒的不同亚型-儿童病毒性毛细支气管炎和肺炎的主要原因。 
患有2型糖尿病，肥胖症和心血管疾病等慢性疾病的患者表现出机能亢进的免疫系统，该系统无法识别无害的刺激，并且与肠道微生物组的改变有关。
 在这些慢性疾病中，肠道微生物组缺乏激活免疫细胞的细菌，这些免疫细胞会阻止肠道对无害细菌的反应。在剖宫产分娩的婴儿，饮食不良的人和老年人中也观察到肠道微生物组的这种改变。
 在美国，1.17亿人（约占成年人口的一半）患有2型糖尿病，肥胖症，心血管疾病或上述疾病的综合症。这表明一半的美国成年人携带有缺陷的微生物组。
 我实验室的研究重点是识别肠道细菌，这些细菌对于建立平衡的免疫系统至关重要，可以抵抗威胁生命的细菌和病毒感染，同时还能耐受我们体内和自身的有益细菌。
 考虑到饮食会影响肠道细菌的多样性，我的实验室研究表明饮食可以用作慢性疾病的治疗方法。人们可以使用不同的食物将肠道微生物组转变为能增强健康免疫反应的微生物组。
 感染SARS-CoV-2（会导致COVID-19疾病的病毒）的患者中，有一小部分会出现严重的并发症，需要在重症监护病房住院治疗。这些患者中有多少有共同点？老年和与饮食相关的慢性疾病，例如肥胖，2型糖尿病和心血管疾病。
 黑人和拉丁人受肥胖，2型糖尿病和心血管疾病的影响尤其严重，所有这些都与营养不良有关。因此，与白人相比，这些人群死于COVID-19的死亡人数并非偶然。不仅在美国，在英国也是如此。 
COVID-19大流行激发了我改变研究方向，并探索了肠道微生物组在针对SARS-CoV-2感染的过度攻击性免疫反应中的作用。
 我和我的同事假设，肥胖，2型糖尿病和心血管疾病等重症SARS-CoV-2患者的肠道微生物组发生改变，加重了急性呼吸窘迫综合征。
 据认为，SARS-CoV-2患者的急性呼吸窘迫综合征（一种危及生命的肺部损伤）是由免疫应答的致命过度反应（称为细胞因子风暴）发展而来，导致细胞不受控制地涌入肺部。在这些患者中，他们自己的不受控制的炎性免疫反应而非病毒本身会导致严重的肺损伤和多器官衰竭，从而导致死亡。
 在最近的一篇综述中描述的几项研究已发现COVID-19患者的肠道微生物组发生了改变。包括Seres Therapeutics，4d Pharma PLC，Evelo Biosciences，VEDANTA bioscience和Finch Therapeutics在内的一些公司最近在癌症，抑郁症和炎症性肠病等疾病疗法方面的工作吸引了投资者的关注。
  为了解决这个问题，我和我的同事招募了COVID-19住院的重度和中度症状患者。我们收集了粪便和唾液样本，以确定肠道和口腔微生物组中的细菌是否可以预测COVID-19的严重程度。鉴定可预测COVID-19疾病临床结果的微生物组标志物是帮助确定需要紧急治疗的患者的优先顺序的关键。
 在尚未进行同行评审的论文中，我们证明了肠道微生物组的组成与患者通常使用的临床特征相比是COVID-19严重程度的最强预测指标。具体来说，我们确定粪便中存在一种细菌-粪肠球菌-是COVID-19严重程度的可靠预测指标。粪肠球菌与慢性炎症有关并不奇怪。
 从粪便中收集的粪肠球菌可以在临床实验室中体外生长。因此，粪大肠埃希菌检测可能是一种经济有效，快速且相对容易的方法，可以识别可能需要更多支持治疗和治疗干预以提高生存机会的患者。 
但是，根据我们的研究尚不清楚，改变的微生物组在对SARS-CoV-2感染的免疫反应中起什么作用。最近的一项研究表明，SARS-CoV-2感染会触发称为T调节细胞的免疫细胞失衡，这对免疫平衡至关重要。
 [获得我们最好的科学，健康和技术故事。订阅《对话》的科学通讯。]
 肠道微生物组中的细菌负责这些T调节细胞的正常活化。因此，像我这样的研究人员需要在更长的时间范围内重复采集患者的粪便，唾液和血液样本，以了解在COVID-19患者中观察到的微生物组改变如何调节COVID-19疾病的严重程度，也许是通过改变T-调节细胞。
 作为一名研究饮食，微生物组和免疫力之间相互作用的拉美裔科学家，我必须强调制定更好的政策以改善获得健康食品的途径的重要性，从而使微生物组更加健康。为黑人和拉丁裔社区设计对文化敏感的饮食干预措施也很重要。虽然优质饮食可能无法预防SARS-CoV-2感染，但它可以治疗与其严重性有关的潜在疾病。
note_en:
You may not know it, but you have an army of microbes living inside of you that are essential for fighting off threats, including the virus that causes COVID-19.
 In the past two decades scientists have learned our bodies are home to more bacterial cells than human ones. This community of bacteria that lives in and on us – called the microbiome – resembles a company, with each microbe species performing specialized jobs but all working to keep us healthy. In the gut, the bacteria balance the immune response against pathogens. These bacteria ensure the immune response is effective but not so violent that it causes collateral damage to the host.
 Bacteria in our guts can elicit an effective immune response against viruses that not only infect the gut, such as  norovirus  and rotavirus, but also those infecting the lungs, such as  the flu virus. The beneficial gut microbes do this by ordering specialized immune cells to produce potent antiviral proteins that ultimately eliminate  viral infections. And the body of a person lacking these beneficial gut bacteria won’t have as strong an immune response to invading viruses. As a result, infections might go unchecked, taking a toll on health.
 I am a microbiologist fascinated by the ways bacteria shape human health.  An important focus of my research is figuring out how the beneficial bacteria populating our guts combat disease and infection.  My most recent work focuses on the link between a particular microbe and the severity of COVID-19 in patients. My ultimate goal is to figure out how to enhance the gut microbiome with diet to evoke a strong immune response – for not just SARS-CoV-2 but all pathogens.
   Our immune defense is part of a complex biological response against harmful pathogens, such as viruses or bacteria. However, because our bodies are inhabited by trillions of mostly beneficial bacteria, virus and fungi, activation of our immune response is tightly regulated to distinguish between harmful and helpful microbes.
 Our bacteria are spectacular companions diligently helping prime our immune system defenses to combat infections. A seminal study found that mice treated with antibiotics that eliminate bacteria in the gut exhibited an impaired immune response. These animals had low counts of virus-fighting white blood cells, weak antibody responses and poor production of a protein that is vital for  combating viral infection and modulating the immune response.
 In another study, mice were fed  Lactobacillus bacteria, commonly used as probiotic in fermented food. These microbes reduced the severity of influenza infection. The  Lactobacillus-treated mice did not lose weight and had only mild lung damage compared with untreated mice. Similarly, others have found that treatment of mice with  Lactobacillus protects against different  subtypes of  influenza  virus and human respiratory syncytial virus – the  major cause of viral bronchiolitis and pneumonia in children.
   Patients with chronic illnesses including Type 2 diabetes, obesity and cardiovascular disease exhibit a hyperactive immune system that fails to recognize a harmless stimulus and is linked to an altered gut microbiome.
 In these chronic diseases, the gut microbiome lacks bacteria that activate  immune cells that block the response against harmless bacteria in our guts. Such alteration of the gut microbiome is also observed in  babies delivered by cesarean section, individuals consuming a poor  diet and the  elderly.
 In the U.S., 117 million individuals – about half the adult population –  suffer from Type 2 diabetes, obesity, cardiovascular disease or a combination of them. That suggests that half of American adults carry a faulty microbiome army.
 Research in my laboratory focuses on identifying gut bacteria that are critical for creating a balanced immune system, which fights life-threatening bacterial and viral infections, while tolerating the beneficial bacteria in and on us.
 Given that diet affects the diversity of bacteria in the gut,  my lab studies show how diet can be used as a therapy for chronic diseases. Using different foods, people can shift their gut microbiome to one that boosts a healthy immune response.
 A fraction of patients infected with SARS-CoV-2, the virus that causes COVID-19 disease, develop severe complications that require hospitalization in intensive care units. What do many of those patients have in common?  Old age and chronic diet-related diseases like obesity, Type 2 diabetes and cardiovascular disease.
 Black and Latinx people are disproportionately affected by obesity, Type 2 diabetes and cardiovascular disease, all of which are linked to poor nutrition. Thus, it is not a coincidence that  these groups have suffered more deaths from COVID-19 compared with whites. This is the case not only in the U.S. but also  in Britain.
   The COVID-19 pandemic has inspired me to shift my research and explore the role of the gut microbiome in the overly aggressive immune response against SARS-CoV-2 infection.
 My colleagues and I have hypothesized that critically ill SARS-CoV-2 patients with conditions like obesity, Type 2 diabetes and cardiovascular disease exhibit an altered gut microbiome that aggravates  acute respiratory distress syndrome.
 Acute respiratory distress syndrome, a life-threatening lung injury, in SARS-CoV-2 patients is thought to develop from a  fatal overreaction of the immune response called a  cytokine storm  that causes an uncontrolled flood  of immune cells into the lungs. In these patients, their own uncontrolled inflammatory immune response, rather than the virus itself, causes the  severe lung injury and multiorgan failures that lead to death.
 Several studies  described in one recent review have identified an altered gut microbiome in patients with COVID-19. And some companies including Seres Therapeutics, 4d Pharma PLC, Evelo Biosciences, VEDANTA bioscience, and Finch Therapeutics have recently attracted investor attention for their work on therapies for diseases including cancer, depression and inflammatory bowel diseases.
  To address this question, my colleagues and I recruited COVID-19 hospitalized patients with severe and moderate symptoms. We collected stool and saliva samples to determine whether bacteria within the gut and oral microbiome could predict COVID-19 severity. The identification of microbiome markers that can predict the clinical outcomes of COVID-19 disease is key to help prioritize patients needing urgent treatment.
 We demonstrated, in a paper which has not yet been peer reviewed, that the composition of the gut microbiome is the strongest predictor of COVID-19 severity compared to patient’s clinical characteristics commonly used to do so. Specifically, we identified that the presence of a bacterium in the stool – called  Enterococcus faecalis– was a robust predictor of COVID-19 severity. Not surprisingly,  Enterococcus faecalis has been associated with  chronic  inflammation.
 Enterococcus faecalis collected from feces can be grown outside of the body in clinical laboratories. Thus, an  E. faecalis test might be a cost-effective, rapid and relatively easy way to identify patients who are likely to require more supportive care and therapeutic interventions to improve their chances of survival.
 But it is not yet clear from our research what is the contribution of the altered microbiome in the immune response to SARS-CoV-2 infection. A recent study has shown that  SARS-CoV-2 infection triggers an imbalance in immune cells called  T regulatory cells that are critical to immune balance.
 [ Get our best science, health and technology stories.  Sign up for The Conversation’s science newsletter.]
 Bacteria from the gut microbiome are responsible for the  proper activation  of those T-regulatory  cells. Thus, researchers like me need to take repeated patient stool, saliva and blood samples over a longer time frame to learn how the altered microbiome observed in COVID-19 patients can modulate COVID-19 disease severity, perhaps by altering the development of the T-regulatory cells.
 As a Latina scientist investigating interactions between diet, microbiome and immunity, I must stress the importance of better policies to improve access to healthy foods, which lead to a healthier microbiome. It is also important to design culturally sensitive dietary interventions for Black and Latinx communities. While a good-quality diet might not prevent SARS-CoV-2 infection, it can treat the underlying conditions related to its severity.
title: 健康的微生物组可建立强大的免疫系统，有助于击败Covid
title_en: A healthy microbiome builds a strong immune system that could help defeat Covid

note:
Facebook拥有的消息传递应用程序WhatsApp已开始向用户发布有关其“对您的隐私的承诺”的状态消息。应用内消息周六在美国和英国的The Verge工作人员中出现，一些用户报告说状态消息-WhatsApp的Snapchat Stories或Twitter Fleets版本已经在印度出现了一段时间。
 WhatsApp发言人在致The Verge的电子邮件中说：“关于我们最近的更新，存在很多误解和困惑，我们希望帮助所有人了解WhatsApp如何保护人们的隐私和安全性的事实。” “展望未来，我们将在“状态”标签中向人们提供更新，以便人们直接从WhatsApp听到信息。我们的第一个更新重申，WhatsApp无法看到您的个人消息，Facebook也看不到，因为它们受到端到端加密的保护。”
 消息显示“不新鲜的一件事就是我们对您的隐私的承诺”，并提醒您“ WhatsApp端到端加密后无法阅读或收听您的个人对话。”
    这些消息是WhatsApp做出的更大努力的一部分，目的是消除对即将更新其隐私政策的误解。该更新旨在说明将WhatsApp用于客户服务的企业如何将其聊天记录存储在Facebook的服务器上。 WhatsApp预览了11月商务聊天的变化。但是，考虑到Facebook的隐私失误历史，用户误解了隐私政策的变更，意味着WhatsApp需要与Facebook共享敏感的个人资料信息。
 该公司发布了有关更改的常见问题页面，并推迟了更新从2月到5月生效的日期。它在本月初发布了一份声明，解决了这一困惑，重申了新隐私政策将涵盖的内容：
 该更新不会改变WhatsApp与Facebook的数据共享方式，也不会影响人们与世界各地的朋友或家人进行私人交流的方式。 WhatsApp始终致力于保护人们的隐私。我们正在通过WhatsApp与用户直接交流有关这些更改的信息，因此他们有时间在下个月的过程中查看新政策
 在随之而来的混乱中，竞争对手的消息应用程序Signal和Telegram最近都看到了新用户的激增。 Telegram上周表示，它增加了用户从WhatsApp导入其聊天记录的功能。 Signal为其应用程序添加了新的主流聊天功能，例如动画贴纸和壁纸。
note_en:
Facebook-owned messaging app WhatsApp has started posting  Status messages to users about its “commitment to your privacy.” The in-app messages were appearing for members of  The Verge staff in the US and UK on Saturday, and some users reported the Status messages — WhatsApp’s version of Snapchat Stories or Twitter Fleets—have been appearing in India for a while now.
 “There’s been a lot of misinformation and confusion around our recent update and we want to help everyone understand the facts behind how WhatsApp protects people’s privacy and security,” a WhatsApp spokesperson said in an email to  The Verge. “Going forward, we’re going to provide updates to people in the Status tab so people hear from WhatsApp directly. Our first update reaffirms that WhatsApp cannot see your personal messages, and neither can Facebook, because they are protected by end-to-end encryption.”
 The messages read “One thing that isn’t new is our commitment to your privacy,” and a reminder that “WhatsApp can’t read or listen to your personal conversations as they’re end-to-end encrypted.”
    The messages are part of a  larger effort from WhatsApp to dispel misperceptions about an upcoming update to its privacy policy. The update is meant to explain how businesses that use WhatsApp for customer service may store logs of their chats on Facebook’s servers. WhatsApp  previewed the changes to business chats in November. Given Facebook’s history of privacy blunders, however, users misinterpreted the changes to the privacy policy to mean WhatsApp would require sharing sensitive profile information with Facebook.
 The company  posted an FAQ page about the changes, and has  pushed back the date the update will take effect from February to May. It issued a statement earlier this month addressing the confusion to reiterate what the new privacy policy would cover:
 The update does not change WhatsApp’s data sharing practices with Facebook and does not impact how people communicate privately with friends or family wherever they are in the world. WhatsApp remains deeply committed to protecting people’s privacy. We are communicating directly with users through WhatsApp about these changes so they have time to review the new policy over the course of the next month
 Amid the ensuing confusion, rival  messaging apps Signal and Telegram have both recently seen a surge in new users. Telegram said last week it’s added the ability for users to  import their chat history from WhatsApp. And  Signal has added new mainstream chat features like animated stickers and wallpapers to its app.
title: WhatsApp使用状态消息尝试向用户保证隐私
title_en: WhatsApp is using Status messages to try to reassure users about privacy

note: 世界各国都看到计算机科学专业学生的数量激增。在2006年至2016年期间，美国和加拿大的相关大学课程入学人数增加了两倍，欧洲的人数也有所增加。同时，开始编码的年龄越来越小，因为许多不同国家的政府都在推动K-12计算机科学教育。尽管计算机编程越来越流行，但我们的大脑如何适应这种相对较新的活动知之甚少。日本研究人员的一项新研究检查了三十位具有不同专业水平的程序员的大脑活动，发现专家程序员的大脑皮层，顶叶和颞皮层的七个区域均经过微调以进行编程。这一发现表明，更高的编程技能是建立在多个分布的大脑区域网络上的微调大脑活动之上的。 ＆＃34;许多研究报告了专家和新手程序员在行为表现，知识结构和选择性注意方面的差异。我们不知道的是这些差异出现在大脑的何处，日本奈良科学技术学院副教授，该研究的主要作者之一高隆久保说。为了回答这个问题，研究人员观察了一些新手，经验丰富的专家级程序员。在对功能MRI（fMRI）进行观察的同时，向程序员显示了72个不同的代码段，并要求他们将每个代码段放入四个功能类别之一。不出所料，具有较高技能的程序员在正确地对代码片段进行分类方面比较擅长。随后的探照灯分析显示，程序员的技能水平增强了七个大脑区域的信息量：双侧下额叶回三角肌（IFG Tri），左下壁小叶（IPL），左上腹回（SMG），左中颞下回（MTG / IT）和右中额回（MFG）。 ＆＃34;在专家程序员中识别这些特征大脑为理解编程专业知识背后的认知机制提供了一个很好的起点。我们的发现阐明了构成编程专业知识的潜在认知功能集合。久保说。更具体地说，已知左IFG Tri和MTG与自然语言处理相关联，尤其是与面向目标的方式的语义知识检索相关联。左IPL和SMG与情节记忆检索相关联。正确的MFG和IFG Tri在功能上与刺激驱动的注意力控制有关。 ＆＃34;编程是人类历史上相对较新的活动，其机制很大程度上未知。将活动与其他知名的人类认知功能联系起来，将提高我们对编程专业知识的理解。如果我们获得有关编程专业知识的更全面的理论，它将导致更好的学习和教授计算机编程方法，＆＃34;久保说。参考：Ikutani Y，Kubo T，Nishida S等。专家程序员已对源代码进行了微调的皮质表示。 eNeuro。 2021; 8（1）。 doi：10.1523 / ENEURO.0405-20.2020本文已从以下材料重新发布。注意：材料的长度和内容可能已被编辑。有关更多信息，请联系引用的来源。
note_en: Countries around the world are seeing a surge in the number of computer science students. Enrolment in related university programs in the U.S. and Canada tripled between 2006-2016 and Europe too has seen rising numbers. At the same time, the age to start coding is becoming younger and younger because governments in many different countries are pushing K-12 computer science education. Despite the increasing popularity of computer programming, little is known about how our brains adapt to this relatively new activity. A new study by researchers in Japan has examined the brain activity of thirty programmers of diverse levels of expertise, finding that seven regions of the frontal, parietal and temporal cortices in expert programmer's brain are fine-tuned for programming. The finding suggests that higher programming skills are built upon fine-tuned brain activities on a network of multiple distributed brain regions.  "Many studies have reported differences between expert and novice programmers in behavioural performance, knowledge structure and selective attention. What we don't know is where in the brain these differences emerge," says Takatomi Kubo, an associate professor at Nara Institute of Science and Technology, Japan, and one of the lead authors of the study.  To answer this question, the researchers observed groups of novices, experienced, and expert programmers. The programmers were shown 72 different code snippets while under the observation of functional MRI (fMRI) and asked to place each snippet into one of four functional categories. As expected, programmers with higher skills were better at correctly categorizing the snippets. A subsequent searchlight analysis revealed that the amount of information in seven brain regions strengthened with the skill level of the programmer: the bilateral inferior frontal gyrus pars triangularis (IFG Tri), left inferior parietal lobule (IPL), left supramarginal gyrus (SMG), left middle and inferior temporal gyri (MTG/IT), and right middle frontal gyrus (MFG).  "Identifying these characteristics in expert programmers' brains offers a good starting point for understanding the cognitive mechanisms behind programming expertise. Our findings illuminate the potential set of cognitive functions constituting programming expertise," Kubo says.  More specifically, the left IFG Tri and MTG are known to be associated with natural language processing and, in particular, semantic knowledge retrieval in a goal-oriented way. The left IPL and SMG are associated with episodic memory retrieval. The right MFG and IFG Tri are functionally related to stimulus-driven attention control.  "Programming is a relatively new activity in human history and the mechanism is largely unknown. Connecting the activity to other well-known human cognitive functions will improve our understanding of programming expertise. If we get more comprehensive theory about programming expertise, it will lead to better methods for learning and teaching computer programming," Kubo says.   Reference:   Ikutani Y, Kubo T, Nishida S, et al. Expert Programmers Have Fine-Tuned Cortical Representations of Source Code.  eNeuro. 2021;8(1). doi: 10.1523/ENEURO.0405-20.2020  This article has been republished from the following  materials. Note: material may have been edited for length and content. For further information, please contact the cited source.
title: 分析员的大脑揭示了不同的编程
title_en: Analysis of Coders' Brain Reveals Different Programing

受到Google Play商店禁止的不合逻辑的最新应用是Element，这是一种适用于联合Matrix聊天协议的开源，端到端加密消息客户端。 Google于周五晚间禁止了Element，Element称此禁令是由于Matrix上某处的滥用内容。 Matrix有数百万用户，作为联合聊天协议，Element不控制Matrix上的内容，因此这有点像禁止使用Web浏览器显示Web内容。 Element表示正在与Google合作，以解释Element的工作原理并解决问题。
 Google一直在打击显示可恨内容的应用程序，但Element表示不应将其作为打击活动的一部分。 ＆＃34;我们还解释了我们作为Element运行的Matrix服务器（包括代表Matrix.org Foundation运行的默认Matrix.org家庭服务器）具有严格的使用条款，我们会积极执行这些条款，＆ ＃34;元素说。 ＆＃34;我们憎恶滥用行为，而Element不是一款迎合滥用内容的应用程序。
  Element（以前称为Riot.im）经常被称为Matrix最好的客户端之一。它看起来很像Slack和Discord，并且实际上可以与Slack和Discord互操作。 Element说，它被法国，德国，英国和美国政府，无数大学，数千家企业和全球数百万人使用。在WhatsApp发布有关Facebook数据共享的公告之后，该应用程序最近吸引了新用户涌入，因此也许引发了Google的更深入审查。截至发稿时，Play商店中仍在激活许多其他Matrix应用程序（按定义显示与Element相同的内容）。
     Google削减了所有Play商店交易的30％，这本来应该用来支付商店运营费用的，但该公司并没有聘请人员像Apple那样主要审查应用，而是选择让机器人处理它。苹果最近将其App Store税从30％降低到15％，因此苹果实际上向开发人员收取的费用要比Google少，同时也提供了更好的人工支持。 Element在撰写本文时对此情况的最新更新表示，已经等待了八个多小时才能收到电子邮件回复。元素说，它没有通知取消，现在已经超过了24小时标记。
  机器人解释Google的规则通常是没有意义的，因此Google经常禁止Play商店中的随机小应用程序，因为它们可以显示Internet上的内容。我们已经看到Reddit应用程序，播客应用程序和其他显示第三方Web内容的应用程序都“赢”了。 Google Play禁止周票，只是因为它们可以显示第三方内容。 Google搜索，Chrome，Gmail，所有Google的聊天应用程序，YouTube，Google云端硬盘和Google播客也是如此，但是这些应用程序从未被禁止。本周早些时候，Google禁止了一个视频应用，因为该应用列出了对标准＆＃34..ass＆＃34;的支持。字幕文件类型。 Play商店的一个好处是您不必使用它，因为Android支持侧载。 Element也位于F-Droid（Android应用程序的全开源存储库）上，尽管该版本提供的版本已经过了一个月。最新版本是Android黑客社区最大的应用商店APKmirror上的版本。
 这些禁令几乎是临时错误的解决方法，这些错误在几个小时或几天后就得到了纠正，但是对于因强制执行错误而突然将其业务从其下撤出的应用程序开发人员来说，却没有什么安慰。 Element说＆＃34;我们只能为因应用程序消失而造成的中断表示歉意。＆＃34;
title: Google Play以“滥用内容”为由禁止开源Matrix客户端元素
